titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Polly Luckett Murray papers
titlestmt.author: Katherine Isham

publicationstmt.publisher: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday March 12, 2026 at 22:00

corpname.part: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

did.abstract: Mary "Polly" Luckett Murray (1934-2019) was an artist and resident of Lyme, Connecticut whose documentation of local illness and advocacy work prompted the medical investigation that identified Lyme disease. After the bacteria and means of transmission were identified she worked to raise public awareness of Lyme disease and authored The Widening Circle: a Lyme disease pioneer tells her story . The collection documents Murray's data collection of symptoms experienced by herself, members of her family, and members of the community; communications with the Connecticut State Department of Health and Dr. Allen C. Steere of the Department of Rheumatology at Yale University School of Medicine; and public awareness work. Materials in the collection include correspondence; files on medical symptoms and treatments; notes; research materials; conference and symposium materials; scrapbooks of newspaper articles (originals and photocopies); photograph albums; slides; VHS, DVD, and cassette tape recordings; Lyme disease related ephemera; and publications.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open for research.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is organized in seven series: Series I. Subject files, 1970-2009. Series II. Murray family medical files, 1964-1993, undated. Series III. Conferences, symposiums,and events 1983–2002. Series IV.The Widening Circle files, circa 1976-1999. Series V. Research binders, 1977-2002. Series VI. Scrapbooks, photograph albums, and slides, 1976-1996, undated. Series VII. Audiovisual materials, 1985–2008.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Mary "Polly" Luckett Murray (1934-2019) was an artist and medical activist, whose investigations contributed to the identification of Lyme disease. Murray was born in New York City in 1934, grew up in Irvington, New York, and completed a BA at Mount Holyoke College in 1954. After graduation, she studied at the Yale School of Art and the Art Students League of New York. She maintained a lifelong artistic practice and exhibited works at many venues including the Museum of American Folk Art, the Lyme Academy, the Old Lyme Art Academy, the Cooley Gallery, the Essex Art Association Gallery, the Clinton Art Society, the Mystic Art Association Gallery, New Haven Paint and Clay Club, and the Shippee Gallery. She married Irvin Gillis Murray and raised four children in Lyme, Connecticut: Alexander "Sandy" Gillis Murray, David Kingman Murray, Caroline "Wendy" Sayles Zeidner, and Todd Ingalls Murray.

During the 1960s and 1970s, Murray's family suffered from a variety of medical symptoms including rashes, joint pain, migraines, and fever. Doctors treating the Murray family denied the possibility that symptoms experienced by different family members might be related to the same source of illness. After she compiled a list of her family's medical symptoms to convince doctors to look for a common cause, she realized many others in the community were suffering from similar symptoms, including an inordinately high rate of juvenile arthritis. In 1975, Murray called the Connecticut State Department of Health with a list of symptoms, including juvenile arthritis, from more than 40 cases of what would later be identified as Lyme disease. The call to the State prompted a meeting between Murray and Dr. Allen C. Steere of the Department of Rheumatology at Yale University School of Medicine. Steere subsequently began studying the pattern of disease documented by Murray and wrote the first medical papers of what was initially described as Lyme arthritis.

Following the identification of Lyme arthritis, later known as Lyme disease, Murray worked with multiple organizations, medical professionals, and government officials to raise public awareness of the disease and secure funding for medical research. She corresponded with researchers and lectured medical students at Case Western Reserve University and Columbia University. She was also deeply involved in Lyme disease support groups and foundations in several states including Connecticut, New York and California. In 1996, Murray published The Widening Circle: A Lyme Disease Pioneer Tells Her Story (St. Martin's Press). Murray continued her Lyme disease advocacy work until shortly before her death from Alzheimer's disease in 2019.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Polly Luckett Murray papers. Historical Library, Harvey Cushing / John Hay Whitney Medical Library, Yale University.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Ms Coll 46 Stephen E. Malawista papers, 1957-2013, Cushing/Whitney Medical Historical Library, Yale University.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mary "Polly" Luckett Murray (1934-2019) was an artist and resident of Lyme, Connecticut whose documentation of local illness and advocacy work prompted the medical investigation that identified Lyme disease. After the bacteria and means of transmission were identified she worked to raise public awareness of Lyme disease and authored The Widening Circle: a Lyme disease pioneer tells her story . The collection documents Murray's data collection of symptoms experienced by herself, members of her family, and members of the community; research into medical conditions related to Lyme disease, other tick-borne diseases, and diseases with symptoms similar to Lyme disease; communications with the Connecticut State Department of Health and Dr. Allen C. Steere of the Department of Rheumatology at Yale University School of Medicine; creating The Widening Circle , and work to raise public awareness of Lyme disease with organizations, conferences, and events.

Series I. Subject files, 1970-2009, includes files created by Polly Murray on a variety of topics related to Lyme disease, including medical research into tick-borne illnesses, correspondence with people suffering from Lyme disease or related illnesses, press coverage, advocacy to raise awareness and funding, and consultations with medical professionals. Murray created a partial index of the contents of the subject files, which may be found in Box 1 folder 1. Correspondence with people suffering from Lyme disease or related illnesses is filed under the title "patients" and organized by date. Files related to Murray's consultations with Allen Steere from the Department of Rheumatology at the Yale University School of Medicine, including lists of symptoms suffered by her family and the community around Lyme Connecticut, are filed under "Steere."

Series II. Murray family medical files, 1964-1993, undated, includes medical records, timelines with lists of symptoms for individuals or the family, correspondence, and written narratives created or collected by Polly Murray regarding herself, her husband Irvin Gillis Murray ("Gil"), and her children: Alexander ("Sandy"), David, Caroline ("Wendy"), and Todd. Additional materials related to consultations with medical providers, including narratives and symptom lists labeled as being created for meetings with Allen Steere, may be found in Series I. Subject files, filed under providers' names.
Series III. Conferences, symposiums, and events 1983–2002. The series includes programs, brochures, informational materials, notes, speeches, transcripts, and correspondence related to conferences, symposiums, and events about Lyme disease. The series is organized by date.

Series IV. The Widening Circle files, circa 1976-1999, includes a variety of materials regarding the creation and publication of Murray's book. Materials include photocopies of medical journal articles and publications, many with post-it notes annotated with footnote numbers and notes referencing sections of the book or with colored dot stickers referencing footnote numbers; correspondence with the publisher; the publication contract; fan mail; press; and sales numbers. Published works in the series are filed under the publication title, other source materials are roughly organized by footnote numbers. Additional materials used for Murray's book may be found in Series I. Subject files, Series V. Research binders, Series VI. Scrapbooks, photograph albums, and slides, and Series VII. Audiovisual materials.

Series V. Research binders, 1977-2002, includes materials, primarily photocopies of journal articles related to Lyme disease, Lyme diseases symptoms, and other tick-borne illness organized by topic. The materials have been removed from original binders and placed in folders for access. The series also includes some correspondence. Colored dot stickers and post-it notes indicate materials used by Murray as sources for The Widening Circle or other writings.

Series VI. Scrapbooks, photograph albums, and slides, 1976-1996, undated, includes scrapbooks compiled by Murray, using original or photocopied articles from newspapers and magazines; a photograph album created by Murray with images of her home, Lyme disease events, medical professionals, and some clippings; an album documenting the Seventh Annual International Conference on Lyme Borreliosis, 1996, including photographs and ephemera; slides used in presentations about Lyme disease and press about Polly Murray; and an oversized photograph of Murray with Ella Grasso. Many pages of scrapbooks include colored dots or post-it notes indicating their use by Murray as material for her book The Widening Circle .

Series VII. Audiovisual materials, 1985–2008, includes recordings on VHS tapes and DVDs of programs regarding Lyme disease aired on broadcast television, some including interviews with Polly Murray, and recordings on a cassette tape from the Fourth International Conference on Lyme Borreliosis, held in Stockholm, Sweden, June 18-21, 1990.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright has been transferred to Yale University for unpublished materials authored or otherwise produced by Polly Luckett Murray. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

title.part: The Widening Circle: A Lyme Disease Pioneer Tells Her Story

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Copy used by Polly Murray, includes post-it notes, some with annotations.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Scrapbook contains clippings about Lyme arthritis, later identified as Lyme disease, from newspapers and magazines, some pages are flagged with post it notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Scrapbook contains photocopies of clippings about Lyme arthritis, later identified as Lyme disease, from newspapers and magazines. Some pages and items are annotated with colored dots or post it notes used by Murray when writing The Ever Widening Circle . Contents of this scrapbook are an almost exact copy of the other 1976-1979 scrapbook in the collection.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Scrapbook contains photocopies of clippings about Lyme disease from newspapers and magazines with some original clippings and correspondence. Some pages and items are annotated with colored dots or post it notes used by Murray when writing The Ever Widening Circle .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Scrapbook contains photocopies of clippings about Lyme disease from newspapers and magazines. Some pages and items are annotated with colored dots or post it notes used by Murray when writing The Ever Widening Circle .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Scrapbook contains photocopies of clippings from newspapers and magazines, with a few original clipping. Some pages and items are annotated with colored dots or post it notes used by Murray when writing The Ever Widening Circle .

did.unittitle: Lyme Borreliosis, Seventh Annual International Conference, June 16-21, 1996

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photograph album gifted to Polly Murray by the Lyme disease Resource Center of Sonoma, California, includes photographs and conference ephemera.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes clippings and photographs with images of Polly Murray; the Murray family; Lyme disease events and presentations, including people with Lyme disease bites and symptoms; medical personnel and spaces; and the property around the Murray family home in Lyme, Connecticut. Materials were removed from original album for preservation.

did.unittitle: Polly Murray with Ella Grasso and others, oversize photograph

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Images include presentations about Lyme disease and photographs of Polly Murray. Also includes a list of "credits for 35 mm transparencies."

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Audiovisual materials are restricted pending reformatting.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Side A contains recording from June 18, 1990 labeled "Eric Asbrink; Nils Thyresson - history of dermatology; Ingrid Garnstorp - patient story; David Persing - antiquity of Lyme disease. Side B contains a recording from June 19, 1990 labeled "Eva Asbrink - course, classification." Post it note on cassette case indicates recordings were used by Polly Murray when writing The Widening Circle .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Panasonic 120 minute tape. Spine label reads: CBS Morning Show Lyme disease 1985, March 1994 Kathryn Hepburn Movie; March 1994 The Making of the Wizard of Oz; March 30 1993 Kay-Gelston House; The Return of Father Goose

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Recoton 120 minute tape. Spine label reads: Lyme disease Channel 8 Dr. Schoen, April 15, 1988, 1-0296; 2359 20/20 arthritis infections, Dr. Brown, Fall 1988; Tape free to 2685; Amish Play May 30; New York MDs (Joan) Rivers,June 3; Lyme, Joan Rivers June 6, 1990.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Recoton 120 minute tape. Spine label reads: Ocean Liners N(ews) 6; Medical research NOVA; Karen April 1989; Inside Addiction repeat, August 16, 1989 (see other tape); NOVA Harvard Med, 1st part, April 1989.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Recoton 120 minute tape. Spine label reads: October 1997 Vietnam, David; Breast Cancer March 29, 1989; NIH Infectious Disease, The Health Century; 20/20 Lyme disease June 30, 1989; July 13, 1989 Nightline, Rahn, Dattywyler, Karen, Durland, Wormser; July 23 Karen; Channel 8, WVIT, Foundation Local Lyme, July 1989.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Recoton 120 minute tape. Spine label reads: NOVA April 1989, 2nd part of Harvard Med; Channel 3 Lyme disease spot May 12, 1989; ...MacNeil/Lehrer NewsHour, cholesterol, September 26, 1989; Greenhouse effect November 29, 1989, show(?) Todd; New England islands, March 1992, part 2.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Recoton 120 minute tape. Spine label reads: February 9, 1990 20/20 Chemo; Shellfish 20/20; Lyme disease Sally Jesse Raphael June 7, 1990; Thai prostitutes June 7, 1990.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Maxell XL hifi 120 minute tape. Spine label reads: Lyme disease, CNN ABC, July 2-7, 1990; N.T.; Polly Murray. Label taped to side of tape reads: CNN-ABC July 2-9; Ch 8 June 1988; Ch 7 July 1989; Ch 7 April 1990; NBC June 1990; Vaccine for dogs Ch 7 July 5, 1990; Dr. Luger Good Morning America, July 6, 1990; 1989 Ch 7 ABC "Home" show with Forshner; long gap; Regis and Kathie Lee; Ch 7 20/20 NYC spot July 1990; I taped 20/20 by mistake sorry, Marilynn; Channel 8 Karen, decrease NT.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Spine label adhered to front of tape reads: 1992 Testing-news releases; May 1992 Rocky Mountain spotted fever; Health Show Karen, Washington 1990, Ed Masters, Jones, Duray; Dogs vaccinations; Staten Island- Rocky Mountain (spotted fever); Satellite press tour October 15, 1992, Karen, New Mexico, Conneaupat Lab, vaccine; Dr. Laura Fisher- preventive drugs- Lyme disease.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Polaroid supercolor T-120 minute tape. Spine label reads: 1995, Jocelyn Elders (?) Dursban insecticide; Norwegian discovery of America; 1995 Perry Como Xmas; 20/20 Pylobacteria-ulcer discovery; Ritalin for hyperactivity - 60 Minutes December 10, 1995.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: TDK 120 minute tape. Spine label reads: Garden D Day; Vietnam child burning; American Impression art history, May 1997; June 97 Niteline, Vietnam girl; Latex Allergy March 14, 1997; Heart Muscle Surgery April 7, 1997; Antiques Roadshow May 1, 1997; Melanoma May 2; Kate Hepburn 90th Bday; PBS May 12 Kate Hepburn; Lyme Disease Yale meeting demo, May 20; Memorial Day Concert.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Fujipro 120 minute tape. Spine label reads: PBS Lyme Disease June 18, 1999, CPTV 1 hour special; Connecticut Public Television; time of Wendy's wedding.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Fuji 120 minute tape. Spine label reads: American Photographer October 12, 1999, 2 3/4 hour; Y2K problems October 26, 1999; Genetic Blindness, Charlie Brown Charles Schlutz October 31, 1999; Steve Schuester new test Lyme disease New Jersey November 29, 1999; April 2000 HMO; Frontline, Boston, Dr. Solomen Dilemma; Isaac Stein violinist April 2008; Ballard Pioneers.

scopecontent.p: Label reads: Termite Art Productions MSTR368, Deadly bugs. Notation on label: Polly. Lyme pioneer, Lock(wood Farm) Ag(ricultural) Sta(tion), one spot beg.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Carrera tape. Spine label reads: Forschers, Mary MD; Betty G. support group interviews; California Steere, Luger, Rahn, Lavoie. Written on one side of paper box: Didn't send to producer in California Christian Taylor. Written on other side of paper box: English 1991, Box Productions, Rheumatoid Arthritis.

did.unittitle: In our own backyard, New Jersey

scopecontent.p: All DVDs in the collections were created by Home Video Studio, Westfield New Jersey.
